channel.title: Programming on d3adb5's personal website
channel.description: Recent content in Programming on d3adb5's personal website

item.title: Why are Ruby blocks constructed like this?
item.description: I took up a position with a new company that involves the use of SaltStack. Since I’d never used it before, I decided to try my hand at setting up a proof of concept, and for that I wanted to use multiple machines. So I decided to use Vagrant, another tool I’m not familiar with, to declaratively spin up a few VMs. This is what I first tried: machines = { master: { box: "bento/ubuntu-22.

item.title: Writing your program in C can make it slower
item.description: If you asked me what I thought of programming about a decade ago, my young, inexperienced, zealot self would have likely told you everybody should be programming in C, so that all programs could be really fast, and that programmers who complained about the language were just too lazy to deal with memory allocation and pointers. How mistaken I was! I’m pretty sure I attributed every bit of slow software or transition to lazy Java programmers:
